meta_title: For Freelancers: content creation to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Solve Debugging Issues with AI Content Creation
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with broken code. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster, document your logic, and ship client projects with speed.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop manually tracing logs. Use Claude Code to automate context analysis and Antigravity to generate documentation that explains your logic. By shifting your workflow to AI-assisted code explanation, you turn 'can't debug' moments into structured, high-velocity development sessions that impress your clients.
content.intro:
The screen is glowing, the deadline is six hours away, and your code is a labyrinth of undefined variables and cryptic stack traces. You feel the familiar anxiety of a project stalling, knowing that every minute spent hunting for a missing semi-colon is a minute you aren't billing. This is the silent killer of the freelance career: the inability to debug effectively under pressure.

But the best developers don't just 'know' the code; they know how to interrogate it. By using AI-driven content generation to document and visualize your logic, you transform the debugging process from a guessing game into a structured engineering workflow. It is time to stop fighting the code and start architecting the solution.

steps.title: Contextualize the Failure
steps.description: Before fixing, you must define the scope of the failure. Use Claude Code to parse your recent logs to identify the exact point of divergence.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided error logs and identify the root cause of the crash in the auth module, focusing on race conditions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Analyze log file and summarize the root cause of the error'

steps.title: Generate Logic Documentation
steps.description: Create a high-level overview of the problematic function. Writing it out forces clarity and allows the AI to spot logical gaps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a technical sequence diagram and pseudocode explanation for the current login flow to identify missing error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file docs/flow_analysis.md --prompt 'Document the logic flow of the current function'

steps.title: Automated Refactoring
steps.description: Apply the insights from your documentation to rewrite the buggy segment. Keep it modular to ensure ease of future testing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the identified function to include robust error handling and type safety, adhering to clean code principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/auth.ts --instructions 'Apply error handling based on the analysis'

steps.title: Unit Test Generation
steps.description: Never trust a fix until it is tested. Generate unit tests that specifically target the edge cases that caused the initial crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create comprehensive Jest unit tests for the refactored function, covering success paths and all edge cases.

steps.title: Client-Ready Technical Summary
steps.description: Turn your debugging journey into a professional status update for your client, demonstrating competence and proactive problem-solving.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a professional update for a client explaining the bug, the fix implemented, and the new safety measures added to prevent recurrence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file communications/update.txt --prompt 'Write a summary of the fix for the client'

pro_tips: Always maintain an 'AI-logs' folder in your repo to store generated documentation for future project audits.
Use Claude Code to generate 'self-healing' comments that explain the 'why' behind complex business logic.
If a bug persists, ask the AI to play 'Devil's Advocate' and break your new code before you commit it.
Map your project's architecture in a Markdown file; feeding this map to Claude Code during sessions drastically increases output accuracy.
Batch your debugging sessions to avoid context switching; spend 30 minutes debugging everything at once rather than one-by-one.
content.next_steps: Integrate these AI-first debugging steps into your CI/CD pipeline to automate quality assurance before deployment.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to turn debugging nightmares into streamlined, high-value delivery cycles. Stop letting bugs dictate your income and start controlling your development velocity. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
